pi_items.verified_value: usa.gdt (claimed but not explicitly verified in public POE5 datasets list)
pi_items.source: Web search of Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition data files, principlesofeconometrics.com, and learneconometrics.com. The POE5 definition files page does not explicitly list a 'usa' dataset. However, Gretl's built-in sample database includes US macroeconomic time series from FRED, and Lee Adkins' POE5 Gretl data package may contain this file.
Task description and setup script confirm this as the intended output path. Path syntax is valid for a Linux system.
Task description specifies 'gdp' (Real GDP). This is a standard macroeconomic variable. Gretl supports ADF tests on named variables.
Task description specifies 4 lags. Gretl ADF command accepts integer lag values. Verified through Gretl command reference documentation.
Task description specifies 'Constant and Trend' for GDP levels. Gretl ADF command uses --c for constant only and --ct for constant and trend. Setup script uses: adf 4 gdp --c --ct
Task description specifies 'in levels' for GDP. No transformation is applied, testing the variable directly.
Task description specifies 'first difference of gdp' (growth). Gretl syntax: diff(gdp)
Task description specifies 4 lags for GDP difference.
Task description specifies 'Constant only' for GDP growth. Setup script uses: adf 4 diff(gdp) --c
Task description specifies 'first difference of gdp'.
Task description specifies 'inf' (Inflation). This is a standard macroeconomic variable.
Task description specifies 4 lags for inflation.
Task description specifies 'Constant only' for inflation. Setup script uses: adf 4 inf --c
Task description specifies 'in levels' for inflation.

pi_items.metadata_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database (via Principles of Econometrics 5th Ed)
pi_items.verified_value: Partially verified: POE5 (Hill, Griffiths, Lim, 5th Edition) data files are derived from FRED and other government sources. Gretl distribution includes US macroeconomic time series from Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis.
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: (1) FRED is maintained by Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis; (2) POE5 has 158 data files from https://principlesofeconometrics.com; (3) Lee Adkins provides POE5 data for Gretl at learneconometrics.com; (4) Gretl documentation states 'gretl distribution contains one sample database, a collection of US macroeconomic time series from the Federal Reserve Bank of St Louis'

privileged_info_summary: The ADF unit root test task uses three valid test specifications with Gretl ADF command syntax: (1) GDP levels with constant+trend, 4 lags (--c --ct flags); (2) GDP difference with constant only, 4 lags (--c flag); (3) Inflation levels with constant only, 4 lags (--c flag). The data source is correctly attributed to Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database via Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition. The output file path is valid. However, the specific dataset file 'usa.gdt' could not be explicitly verified in public POE5 data listings, though it is consistent with Gretl's practice of including US macroeconomic sample data and aligns with available POE5 data packages.
